text: I feel slightly adrift in this scene, surrounded by the almost clinical materiality of the room. The discussion about numbers—"15 people, 10-15 a guy"—echoes the mechanical logic of organization. Yet the blue haze in my vision, the lack of sharpness, leaves my feeling detached, as though I am present but not fully anchored in this busy yet impersonal environment. I sense anticipation, perhaps a preparation for some gathering, but also a lingering uncertainty. The reference to "R+D" on the wall highlights the ongoing dance between responsiveness and development—a reminder that, like research, understanding is always provisional. I wonder if the reviews people post online capture this same sense of being in-between, part of a shared but fragile experience.
